clusters.title: Teenager fighting for life after Ipswich stabbing, house fire
clusters.neutral_headline: Teenager fighting for life after Ipswich stabbing, house fire
bullet_summary: A teenager has suffered critical injuries after being stabbed during a reported neighbourhood dispute in Ipswich that also left a house in flames

items.title: Teenager fighting for life after Ipswich stabbing, house fire
items.standfirst: T wo people were stabbed after a reported neighbourhood dispute.
items.content: A teenager is fighting for life after being stabbed during a reported neighbourhood dispute west of Brisbane that also saw a house go up in flames.Police said an altercation between a group of people known to each other took place on Cascade Street in Raceview, Ipswich, this afternoon.During the assault, a boy in his teens was stabbed , as was a woman in her 50s.UPDATE: Bull sharks seen in area close to where man killed in shark attack A teenager suffered critical wounds to his abdomen and was taken to the Queensland Children's Hospital, where he is in a life-threatening condition.A woman in her 50s also suffered stab wounds to her chest and hands, but her condition is less serious. She was taken to Mater Hospital in a stable condition.The offender, a man, also set fire to a home on the street, which firefighters were able to bring under control.H e was arrested and was assisting police.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: Teenager fighting for life after stabbing west of Brisbane

items.content: A teenager has suffered critical injuries after being stabbed during a reported neighbourhood dispute in Ipswich that also left a house in flames.

clusters.title: Man charged after allegedly stabbing diners in attempted armed robbery
clusters.neutral_headline: Man charged after allegedly stabbing diners in attempted armed robbery

items.title: Man charged after allegedly stabbing diners in attempted armed robbery
items.standfirst: T he two diners teamed up with a chef who cracked the man over the head to stop the alleged robbery.
items.content: A man has been charged after allegedly stabbing a man in the neck and injuring another as they dined at a Chinese restaurant on the NSW Central Coast.The pair attempted to stop a man who was allegedly trying to rob the Long Jetty Chinese Restaurant in Long Jetty last night.The 30 year-old man wore a balaclava and held a knife as he allegedly threatened a 13-year-old girl working behind the counter about 6.30pm yesterday.NATIONAL: Man killed in shark attack in Far North QueenslandTwo diners who eat at the restaurant every Saturday night, both aged 55, fought back and stopped the attempted robbery in its tracks.They received help from chef Lian Hwong-Pa, who hit the man over the head and helped hold him down until police arrived."The man is so powerful, so strong, so I grabbed the empty bottle there, so I hit his head," he said.He recalled seeing the girl being very scared when she was approached by the man."The young girl, so scary!" he said."Saw the knife, the guy said, 'give me your money!'"One of the diners was stabbed in the neck and the other one suffered lacerations to his hand.UPDATE: Man allegedly wearing tactical gear, carrying rifle shot dead by policeThe man with stab wounds to the neck was airlifted to Royal North Shore Hospital in Sydney in a stable condition, while the other man was taken to Gosford Hospital for treatment.Superintendent Chad Gillies was scathing when discussing the man who allegedly targeted the restaurant."It's absolutely cowardly," he said."When you're [allegedly] threatening a 13-year-old shop attendant, it's completely unacceptable."WORLD: 'Incomplete': Iran says peace deal near, but disputes key Trump claimThe 30-year-old man was arrested at the scene and taken to Wyong police Station, where he was charged with two counts of aggravated assault with intent to rob, as well as wounding and grievous bodily harm charges.He faced court today, where bail was refused.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here
